case_title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRAYLOR TRUST, DATED MAY 3, 1996, AS PURPORTEDLY AMENDED AND RESTATED

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Feb-02-2026 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cameren Ripoli and Justin Hedemark for petitioner Sheila Hicks (via Zoom).
Daniel Leahy for Darryl Lewis, administrator of the Estate of Tamarror Traylor (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on case management and trial setting.

The Court addressed substitution of Darryl Lewis as respondent following the death of Tamarror Traylor. Mr. Ripoli stated that Mr. Lewis would substitute in as respondent and suggested proceeding by stipulation and proposed order, which the Court indicated was appropriate.

Discussion was held regarding the status of the case. Mr. Ripoli stated that petitioner was open to further mediation. Mr. Leahy stated that a prior mediation had been unsuccessful and expressed doubt that the matter would resolve without trial.

Mr. Leahy orally requested an order requiring that proceeds from the sale of any trust real property be deposited into a blocked account. Mr. Hedemark noted that one trust property located in San Francisco was not being sold. Following discussion, the Court ordered that proceeds from the sale of any trust real property be placed into a blocked account and directed Mr. Leahy to submit a proposed order, approved as to form by other counsel.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m., in Department 204. The Court directed the parties to file a joint case management statement.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Continued To Feb-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Justin Hedemark and James Andrade, for Sheila Hicks, Petitioner.
Daniel Leahy, for Darryl Lewis, Administrator of the Estate of Tamarror Traylor.

At the outset, the court and counsel discuss the plan with this matter and related estate matter. Mr. Leahy believes that Ms. Hicks is unfit to act as Trustee and requests the court to order that the Trust property not be sold until matters are resolved; to order that there be no access to Trust funds to pay for litigation; to appoint a neutral fiduciary; and set trial. The court acknowledges Mr. Leahy's requests and advises that it will take no actions on the requests as they should be formally filed. The court instructs counsel to meet and confer regarding the preceding and the matters will be addressed at the next calling. There is discussion regarding Mr. Andrade possibly filing an amended petition. The court advises that it authorizes him to file a petition without leave to amend. No objection to the preceding is presented.

After additional discussion, all subject matters are continued to Feb-02-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file a joint Case Management Statement one week prior.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Robles. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear (To Trustee Tamarror Traylor And Attorney Daniel Leahy)
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark and James Andrade for petitioner Sheila Hicks (via Zoom).
Daniel Leahy for respondent Tamarror Traylor (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on 1) Order to Appear issued to attorney Daniel Leahy; and 2) Order to Show Cause why Tamarror Traylor should not be suspended as trustee and interim trustee appointed by the court.

The Court was advised that Tamarror Traylor had died intestate within the last two weeks. Mr. Leahy reported her only child intends to seek appointment as special administrator of her estate and will require court approval of a contingency fee agreement to pursue the litigation.

Mr. Andrade argued that Ms. Hicks is the named successor trustee under the trust and should assume office. Mr. Leahy argued that appointment of an independent professional fiduciary would be more appropriate. The Court declined to grant such relief absent a properly noticed petition.

The Court encouraged the parties to meet and confer as to next steps.

Following discussion, the Court took the Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause off calendar.

Mr. Leahy was given Court approval to set Petitions for Letters of Special Administration and to Approve Contingency Fee Agreement for hearing on October 13, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Justin Hedemark and James Andrade for Sheila Hicks, Petitioner/Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Daniel Leahy for and with Tamarror Traylor, Objector/Trustee (via Zoom).

Mr. Andrade stated that they are still waiting for subpoenas to be returned, that they are waiting on the tax depositions, that there is outstanding discovery matters, and the possibility of another mediation with parties.

Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Continued To Jun-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark and James Andrade for Sheila Hicks, Petitioner/Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Daniel Leahy for and with Tamarror Traylor, Objector/Trustee (via Zoom).

The court noted a recent case management statement filed the previous day, which outlined ongoing discovery efforts. Mr. Andrade clarified that subpoenas need to be domesticated in Texas for access to certain records and that the process could take approximately 60 days, followed by depositions within another 30-60 days.

The court questioned whether additional ADR would be beneficial. Mr. Leahy stated that he did not believe further ADR would be beneficial, arguing that the matter has already progressed at a glacial pace with limited discovery, and requested a discovery cutoff date to move the case forward. Mr. Andrade stated he was open to ADR, but questioned how a discovery cut-off date could be determined without a set trial date. The court agreed that a discovery cut-off should be linked to a trial date, which would be determined at a future case management conference.

The court continued the matter to June 30, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow for further discovery. A further case management conference was also set for the same date. The parties were ordered to file a supplemental joint case management statement one week prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Dec-04-2023 Continued To Mar-25-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Justin Hedemark, Esq., for Sheila Hicks, Petitioner.
Daniel Leahy, Esq., for and with Tamarror Traylor, Objector.

Counsel presented individual reports to the court regarding mediation efforts. Mr. Leahy requests that the court issue a Breslin order. The court acknowledges Mr. Leahy's request. Mr. Hedemark reports as to what he and his client are expecting from opposing counsel, in order for the mediation to be meaningful.

At conclusion of discussion, the subject matters are continued to Mar-25-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises that it intends to issue a Breslin order which will include a deadline for the parties to complete mediation within ninety (90) days of today, Mar-04-2024. No objection to the preceding is provided. The parties are instructed to file a joint statement re the status of mediation by Mar-14-2024.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Pliego/Singh. Not Reported.
